title: “This UFO Shut Down 10 Nukes!” - Air Force Officer Robert Salas
summary: First Lieutenant Robert Salas recounts his experience at Malmstrom Air Force Base in March 1967, where he witnessed 10 nuclear missiles go offline during a UFO sighting. The interview covers multiple similar incidents at nuclear bases between 1966-1967, discusses the Air Force's attempted cover-ups including the Condon investigation whitewash, and addresses a recent Wall Street Journal hit piece attempting to discredit Salas's testimony.

acronyms.definition: The Air Force command responsible for nuclear weapons and strategic bombers during the Cold War

acronyms.expansion: Air Force Office of Special Investigation
acronyms.definition: Air Force investigative agency that handled sensitive matters including UFO-related non-disclosure agreements
Enlisted military personnel who serve as topside security guards
A burst of electromagnetic energy that can disable electronic equipment
External electromagnetic signals that can disrupt electronic systems
Organization that monitors the skies for possible incoming threats to the US
The location where missiles are actually housed, separate from the launch control center

organizations_mentioned: Air Force Office of Special Investigation

events.description: Minot Air Force Base incident where 10 missiles were shut down during UFO activity, witnessed by David Schindele
Echo Flight incident at Malmstrom AFB on March 16th where 10 missiles shut down during UFO sightings
Oscar Flight incident at Malmstrom AFB on March 24th where Robert Salas witnessed 10 missiles shut down during UFO sighting
Belt, Montana UFO landing witnessed by truck driver, highway patrol, county sheriffs, and investigated by Lt. Col. Lewis Chase
Air Force shut down Project Blue Book citing Condon investigation findings
NORAD report of UFO sightings over Malmstrom missile bases
Retired missile technician John Mills and colleagues saw UFO hovering around Malmstrom, base reported being swarmed
Robert Salas contacted his commander Fred Maywald and learned he was at Oscar Flight, not Echo Flight

key_claims.claim: Between September 1966 and March 1967, 30 nuclear missiles were lost to UFO activity across multiple incidents
10 Minuteman missiles at Oscar Flight shut down one by one while a red-orange pulsating UFO hovered over the facility
Boeing investigation concluded the shutdowns were caused by an external signal but could not explain how that signal got into the triply-shielded system
The Condon investigation was a pre-planned whitewash, with Robert Low writing a memo stating it would appear scientific but find nothing
Lieutenant Colonel Lewis Chase denied knowledge of UFO-related missile shutdowns to Condon investigator Roy Craig despite having written official reports about UFO activity
There were no nuclear tests on March 16th or March 24th 1967, ruling out EMP from nuclear testing as a cause
The EMP model cited by Wall Street Journal as explanation was not operational until 1971 and not ready until 1973
Fred Maywald, Salas's commander, confirmed the essential details of the incident in a recorded conversation
Air Force Office of Special Investigation agents required witnesses to sign non-disclosure agreements after UFO-nuclear incidents
Robert Hastings documented 167 Q-cleared witnesses in his book UFOs and Nukes
